title: Asukas loyalty
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's loyalty             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sat Jul 26, 2014 10:30 am Some fans claim that Shinji didn't help Asuka when she most needed (MP EVAs and mind raping) and she herself claims that "he didn't hold her" (Kiss scene). I wonder if the situation was reverse... - If Shinji were the one who was in the volcano would Asuka try to save him? - If Shinji were the one who was getting his mind raping would Asuka try to save him? - If Shinji were the one in the battle against the MP EVAs would Asuka try to help him? I know that Shinji didn't save her because he couldn't do so (Bakelite and Unit 01 unavalible) but if we exclude these situations where he was unable to do something he would for sure help Asuka in times of need. Now, if the roles were reversed, would Asuka want and try to help her fellow pilot? I know that Asuka served as shield in the battle against Matarael but she was doing this becuase the fact Shinji saved her from sinking in the volcano was a debt to her. I don't hate Asuka I just find her attitudes very doubtful.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jul 26, 2014 10:46 am First of all, it's her job to support him, as it is his job to support her. The only time that not supporting the other in combat is acceptable is if one is under reasonable amounts of stress or tribulation, which actually happens quite frequently in the show. If it were Asuka pre-episode 19, then she would most certainly jump in to help Shinji; however, after ep 19 and her later breakdown in 22, it's not likely that Asuka would be in any position to help anyone (as ep 23 demonstrates). So, really, it's not as simple as helping one another, since everybody has their own issues holding them back. Everyone could honestly use a helping hand, and there simply aren't very many available within the context of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Jul 26, 2014 11:01 am I would see those statements of Asuka's as an unconscious acceptance or expectation that, no matter how bad things got, Shinji would be there to bail her out. As you pointed out, Shinji had Asuka's back a lot, much to her resentment. At a certain point, however, that became the pattern: something would go sideways, and Shinji would somehow stabilize it. The one time he couldn't help was when Asuka actually acknowledged and (in her way) asked for his help. That's an Eva-irony if ever there was one.

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sat Jul 26, 2014 12:30 pm @ Sachi and @ God Hobblin : What I meant was: Shinji feels very worried with Asuka when she's in danger and even when he can't he wants to save her. What I'm questioning is Asuka's ungrateful attitude. What I doubt is: Would Asuka be worried, despite being her job, with Shinji if he was in danger especially when she couldn't save him? Becuase we all know that Shinji is attracted to Asuka but is Asuka attratced to Shinji in her own psychotic way? Or she just see him as a sexual tool (let's put this way) or a substitution for Kaji? In fact, it's her obligation to save her comrades, but I'm sure she would do it grudgingly unlike Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sat Jul 26, 2014 2:17 pm Didn't she tried to during the Leilel attack? She was at the very least as concerned as Rei (whom by the way, was displaying emotions/concern like for the first time) Latter she was bragging about his actions because she could not accept it openly, but wasn't she by the Entry Plug when Misato opened it? In short, I think she would have... Maybe for concern, maybe at least to hold it against him later on...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 26, 2014 2:26 pm The rest is too circumstantial to really ponder, but I don't think she would have jumped in the volcano to save him. Nor should she, because let's face it... that's a pretty damn reckless thing to do and Nerv got extremely lucky they didn't both die because of that stunt. Not that it would ever happen anyway because unit 01 is important and Asuka's whole purpose at Nerv is to be an expendable shock trooper, so OF COURSE she's the one they selected for volcano duty.

[]:             Quote Post by Thomas68 » Sat Jul 26, 2014 5:16 pm SEELE-01 wrote: Latter she was bragging about his actions because she could not accept it openly, but wasn't she by the Entry Plug when Misato opened it?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jul 26, 2014 10:50 pm What do they really owe to another? They're random strangers, with no common friends or interests(Asuka positively hates Touji and Kensuke, be it justified or otherwise), expected to do the same jobs. They're already unhealthyly fixated on their pilot jobs, and piloting is all they have in common... They don't know SHIT about each other, aside from piloting, and even then, they had very different coexistence experiences... Shinji was mostly put into ethical dillemas, while Asuka mostly felt her reason for being there called into question. They understand NOTHING about each other, and that is THE POINT. Basic coexistence =/= twu wuv.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 27, 2014 3:57 am ^ But they try to understand each other, and in EoE finally manage to do it and realize that they aren't that different, that too was the whole point.

[]:             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Sun Jul 27, 2014 4:26 am I think she would based on her ideas (up to the breakdown point) Asuka defines herself by how good she is at her job... much as she might want it she knows she can't do it all alone and has to work in a team. Doesn't she refer to her and the others as "us", even if she competes with the other pilots for recognition and satisfaction... her relationship with the others is complex but she can be logical about it and she knows they need to help each other out. There are moments when she acts as dumb as Shinji has been known to in a battle, though, yea. I am guessing but if she equates having to be saved with being inferior or weak, then it follows saving Shinji would boost her ego and he would owe her, and tbh if she was told to let Shinji die or something I think she'd argue against it and try to save him. Because of this: "What are you, stupid? if someone is attacking you of course you have to fight them!" Just suggests even if on the surface she's competitive and childish, underneath the Angels are "them" and humans and pilots are "us" and of all the pilots in the TV version I think she has the strongest sense of that, and that her purpose is as a soldier to protect humanity. For her it goes without saying.... and Shinji is a human, a classmate, a housemate or part of her psuedo-family, as much as he is a 'rival' and baka. You know, like how people just shove their differences aside a lot of the time during times of danger and crisis and help complete strangers. She would help him, letting him die wouldn't be in her character and would be a huge failure and trauma for them as a team. At least not before her major flip-out. Out of all of them she's the only one I could picture saying to herself "death is not an option" and believing it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 27, 2014 11:12 am ^ I think something important needs to be precised: Asuka isn't against having teammates, as long as they are her subordinates . Her problem is that she wants to be the top member of the Eva pilot squad, the leader, the red ranger who's always in the spotlight. She doesn't have many problems with Rei and Shinji when she can boss them around like a sergeant, see the fight against Matarael when she took command of the squad. Failing that, she doesn't have a problem when the two others are reduced to a support role letting her deal the killing blow to the Angel and be the star (Sahaquiel), it's when the other pilots reach her perceived level of importance that she have a problem, when the command center consider Shinji or Rei's security above her own, or when they got to get the lion share of slaying the Angel. And even then, pre-breakdown, she wasn't so indifferent that she would simply ignore a fellow human being at a risk, she's not Gendo!

[]:             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Mon Jul 28, 2014 8:15 am Yeah, pretty much I guess. As long as she had that personal worth to herself and others, she would always help Shinji. She has no true reason not to, until the breakdown happens and she loses all of her will to do anything or help herself even. It's Shinji who has those sort of issues of "should I/shouldn't I do _____" on the deeper moral level. I don't think Asuka's troubled with those so much. Asuka wants to be a hero, unlike Shinji, so doing heroic stuff should come more naturally to her?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jul 28, 2014 9:09 am Ieyasu wrote: It's Shinji who has those sort of issues of "should I/shouldn't I do _____" on the deeper moral level. I don't think Asuka's troubled with those so much. Asuka wants to be a hero, unlike Shinji, so doing heroic stuff should come more naturally to her?
